data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-80d4bf41">
    <title>auto-route: enumerate per-tier candidates for multi-tier subscription harnesses (claude opus/sonnet/haiku)</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Under policy=default (auto-route mode, no --harness/--provider/--model pin), Fizeau collapses each subprocess harness to ONE scoring candidate — its DefaultModel. Multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (which advertises sonnet/opus/haiku as siblings under one auth) therefore only ever offer their default tier to the auto-route scorer. Sonnet and haiku can never win on cost vs opus even though the catalog has full power/cost metadata for all three.

Concrete symptom from downstream DDx caller: every policy=default dispatch lands on claude/opus-4.7 because internal/harnesses/registry.go:51 sets DefaultModel="opus-4.7". For a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer, opus won score 100.5. When the same dispatch is rerun with --model sonnet-4.6 pinned, sonnet scores 196.0 — it would have beaten opus by 95.5 points if it had been enumerated.

Cost gap is material: opus is 5x sonnet on input tokens, ~5x on output (cost_input_per_m: 15.0 vs 3.0; cost_output_per_m: 75.0 vs 15.0 per internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml). A project running ddx work continuously is defaulting every dispatch to the most expensive tier.

ROOT CAUSE (tentative — pointers, not patch)
service_routing.go:1172-1189 (auto-route candidate add loop):

    for _, h := range entries {
        if h.DefaultModel != "" {
            add(h.DefaultModel, true, status)      // adds opus-4.7 for claude
        }
        for _, modelID := range h.SupportedModels {
            add(modelID, true, status)              // SHOULD add sonnet-4.6 too
        }
        ...
    }

subprocessHarnessModelIDs("claude", cfg) at service_models.go:91-101 returns the full ["sonnet", "sonnet-4.6", "opus", "opus-4.7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"] set. So sonnet SHOULD be reaching the candidate pool. Evidence says it does not — three candidate causes I could not disambiguate without deeper reading of routing.Inputs construction:
1. Downstream aggregation/dedup is keeping one model per {harness, provider} key (picking DefaultModel as representative).
2. Eligibility map keyed by modelID at ~line 1170 is collapsing sonnet entries by family before the candidate list is emitted.
3. h.SupportedModels is being populated empty at the call site that builds entries (different from the metadata_billing.go:71 path).

The routing_decision event is unambiguous: no sonnet row at all in the candidates array, not even excluded. A row with eligible:false, filter_reason:... would be diagnostic; silent absence is consistent with "never enumerated."

CATALOG IS NOT THE GAP
internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml has complete sonnet-4.6 metadata: power 8 (vs opus 10), cost_input_per_m 3.0, cost_output_per_m 15.0, surfaces include agent.anthropic:sonnet-4.6 and claude-code:sonnet-4.6. This rules out the power_missing exclusion path (the one that drops openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 with filter_reason: "power_missing").

SECOND BUG SURFACED BY THE REPRO
Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 routes through fiz/openrouter (catalog's openrouter_id), NOT through the claude subscription harness. So pinning by model name does not produce a same-harness alternative tier even when one exists on the same auth. The openrouter route then fails 401 because no auth header is configured. So the workaround "pin sonnet by name" doesn't actually deliver sonnet on the claude subscription path; it falls back to a separate per-token provider.

PROPOSED FIX (direction, not prescriptive)
For multi-tier subscription harnesses, enumerate one candidate per tier (opus, sonnet, haiku) with each tier's catalog power/cost, all routing through the same harness. Let cost-aware scoring pick the cheapest tier that meets power_hint_fit for the prompt. Today the harness behaves like "one model, take it or leave it"; it should behave like "one auth, multiple tiers."

If aggregation-by-harness is intentional for some other reason, the alternative is to make the harness configurable to expose multiple DefaultModels per role/power-band, and have callers pass a power hint that selects the right one.

REPRO
1. Configure Fizeau with the claude harness (subscription path, no model pin).
2. Send any execute request with policy=default, no --harness/--provider/--model.
3. Inspect the routing_decision event — only claude/opus-4.7 appears under claude; no sonnet, no haiku.
4. Resend with --model sonnet-4.6. Route goes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 with score 196.0 (not to the claude subscription path), and fails 401 if openrouter is not authed.

CALLER CONTEXT
DDx CLI on main branch at commit 09881f3a5. DDx does not pre-resolve routing knobs (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010) and passes policy=default through verbatim. Verified by grep: no routing_decision/power_hint_fit/score_components emitter in DDx — only in github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.12.2.

NON-SCOPE
- The openrouter 401 (separate Fizeau config issue: that route needs an auth header configured).
- DDx-side changes; ddx work is doing the right thing per contract.
- Catalog metadata additions (sonnet-4.6 metadata is already complete).

CROSS-REF
Github issue: https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6 (mirrors this bead for external visibility).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Under policy=default with no --model pin, a claude subscription harness produces multiple candidates in routing_decision.candidates: one per available tier (at minimum opus-4.7 and sonnet-4.6; haiku if catalog has it). Verified by integration test that dispatches a default-policy request and asserts >1 claude candidate.
2. Each per-tier candidate carries the correct power and cost from internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (sonnet=8, opus=10), and each routes to the same harness (claude), not via fiz/openrouter fallback.
3. For a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with policy=default, sonnet-4.6 beats opus-4.7 on score under default scoring weights (cost_input_per_m: 3 vs 15 dominates the cost component). Verified by a deterministic scoring test or a snapshot of the routing_decision event with the chosen tier asserted.
4. Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against the claude subscription harness routes through the claude harness (not fiz/openrouter). Verified by a test that sets up a claude harness mock and asserts dispatch destination.
5. The routing_decision event for any excluded same-harness tier carries an explicit filter_reason — silent absence of a candidate row is forbidden when the tier is known to the catalog. Verified by negative test: artificially break sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata and assert it appears with filter_reason:"power_missing" (or similar) rather than vanishing.
6. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
7. No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests).
    </acceptance>
    <labels>kind:bug, area:routing, area:harness, impact:cost, evidence:downstream-ddx, refs:github-issue-6, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="fe31c062456478c3de7bc9d81fb254542ed965bb" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260516T174611-788fbe14"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

candidates.reason: model power 10 exceeds max_power=5 while an in-bounds candidate exists
model power 10 exceeds max_power=5 while an in-bounds candidate exists
provider bragi endpoint unreachable (aliveness probe failed)

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

data.error: openai: Post "http://bragi:8020/v1/chat/completions": dial tcp 100.127.38.115:8020: i/o timeout
agent: provider error: openai: Post "http://bragi:8020/v1/chat/completions": dial tcp 100.127.38.115:8020: i/o timeout
